pi_items.verified_value: X-ray/Ventilator Synchronization demo application is a real clinical demonstration application in OpenICE Supervisor
pi_items.source: MD PnP official website (mdpnp.org) states: 'The demo application supervisor comes with a sample PCA infusion safety application, simulated medical device controller, a x-ray / ventilator synchronization demo and vital signs visualization application.' This confirms x-ray demo exists; exact name may be 'X-ray/Ventilator Synchronization' rather than 'Xray Viewer' but application functionality for medical imaging integration is verified.

pi_items.verified_value: ICU staff education and orientation is the correct clinical context for OpenICE
pi_items.source: OpenICE is designed for medical device interoperability in clinical settings; MD PnP documentation emphasizes patient safety and clinical use; demonstrated in task description and README context
Verified in verifier.py line 29: PASS_THRESHOLD = 65; confirmed in task.json metadata field
Verified in export_result.sh line 46: GUIDE_FILE="/home/ga/Desktop/clinical_guide.txt"; confirmed in task.json metadata and setup_task.sh cleanup logic

privileged_info_summary: The clinical_app_tour task requires an agent to conduct a structured demonstration of OpenICE clinical applications for ICU staff education. The task requires: (1) creating a Multiparameter Monitor simulated device adapter, (2) launching four clinical demonstration applications - Vital Signs, Xray Viewer (X-ray/Ventilator Synchronization demo), Patient ID, and Infusion Safety, and (3) writing a clinical education guide to /home/ga/Desktop/clinical_guide.txt with dedicated sections for each application covering clinical problems addressed, required device data, clinical use, and OpenICE interoperability benefits. Pass threshold is 65 points. All required devices and applications are authentic components of the OpenICE open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP program at Massachusetts General Hospital.
